answer:
\boxed{B}

The One-Child Policy (1979–2015) directly led to a **sharp decline in China’s total fertility rate**, dropping well below the replacement level of ~2.1 children per woman. While it contributed to slower population growth, China’s population remains large due to its massive existing base. Other options (e.g., out-migration, rural-to-urban shifts) were pre-existing trends, not direct consequences of the policy.
